commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Contributor real-account proof is required, and maintainers need to decide or document the Cursor.app token-source precedence before merge; automation cannot provide that proof.; Needs attention: The diff has no supply-chain change, but it broadens Cursor credential handling by reading Cursor.app's local bearer token without docs or explicit opt-in.; Review finding: [P1] Keep app auth behind browser-cookie im... (sha=1b1416b3edb017a7111f8548ca8f584636f167ed)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Cursor.app SQLite auth fallback, Cursor DashboardService usage/account fetches, and CursorStatusProbe regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. for the remaining review finding: source inspection shows the new Cursor.app auth block runs before browser cookie import while current docs put browser import ahead of stored fallbacks. I did not run live Cursor probes because the repository policy avoids validation that may touch real credentials or prompt for account access.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor real-account proof is required, and maintainers need to decide or document the Cursor.app token-source precedence before merge; automation cannot provide that proof.

target.title: fix: use Cursor app auth as fallback
target.body:
## Summary
- Add a Cursor.app local-auth fallback when browser cookies or manual cookie auth do not yield a usable Cursor session.
- Use Cursor DashboardService endpoints with the local auth token to fetch current-period usage and account info.
- Cover the fallback path with a regression test.

## Test Plan
- [x] `swift test --filter CursorStatusProbeTests` — 29 tests passed
target.author: jackie-qin

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
